steps.title: Identify the land composition and building percentages
steps.content: The plot is 52.0% farmland and 48.0% pasture. Buildings occupy 23.5% of the farmland and 16.0% of the pasture. To find the total percentage $p$ with buildings, we need to account for buildings in each region separately, then combine them.

steps.title: Calculate buildings on farmland as a percentage of the total plot
steps.content:
Buildings on farmland = 23.5% of farmland = 23.5% of 52.0% of the total plot.

$$0.235 \times 0.520 = 0.1222$$

This equals 12.22% of the total plot.

steps.title: Calculate buildings on pasture as a percentage of the total plot
steps.content:
Buildings on pasture = 16.0% of pasture = 16.0% of 48.0% of the total plot.

$$0.160 \times 0.480 = 0.0768$$

This equals 7.68% of the total plot.

steps.title: Add the two portions to find the total building percentage
steps.content:
The total percentage of the plot with buildings is:

$$p = 12.22\% + 7.68\% = 19.90\%$$

Therefore, $p = 19.9$.
